features.description: The project can produce GitHub-style unsigned macOS Release packages from SemVer-tagged commits.
acceptance: Document the SemVer, Git tag, and GitHub Release asset workflow.
Provide a repeatable script that builds an unsigned Release app.
Generate both .dmg and .zip assets under Dist/.
Generate SHA256SUMS.txt and GitHub-style RELEASE_NOTES.md.
Reject formal releases when MARKETING_VERSION, the current tag, or the git worktree state is invalid.
Allow clearly labeled local dev packaging before a release tag exists.
features.notes: Added Docs/release-process.md and Scripts/package-release.sh. The release process documents SemVer, v<MARKETING_VERSION> tags, GitHub Release assets, Gatekeeper warnings, checksum verification, and no-retag safety rules. The packaging script reads MARKETING_VERSION, CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION, and PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER from Config/Release.xcconfig, enforces SemVer, rejects formal releases from dirty worktrees or non-matching tags, supports local --allow-untagged dev packages, builds Release with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO, verifies app bundle identifier/version/build metadata, ad-hoc signs the full app bundle for local resource-seal integrity with a stable local designated requirement based on the bundle identifier, verifies codesign --deep --strict before packaging, rejects pure cdhash designated requirements so Screen Recording TCC grants are not tied to a per-build hash, detects architecture, and creates .dmg, .zip, SHA256SUMS.txt, and RELEASE_NOTES.md under Dist/. Follow-up fix for v0.1.2-alpha Gatekeeper 'app is damaged' dialog: the previous package had only executable-level ad-hoc/linker signing and no sealed resources, causing codesign verification to fail with 'code has no resources but signature indicates they must be present'. Follow-up fix for v0.1.3-alpha Screen Recording permission loop: the previous pure ad-hoc release had designated requirement cdhash-only, which produced duplicate Parrot.app entries in System Settings and allowed users to enable an old hash while the current app remained unauthorized. Verified bash -n Scripts/package-release.sh, git diff --check, full Scripts/package-release.sh --allow-untagged Release build, shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS.txt for the generated dmg and zip, mounted the generated DMG and verified Parrot.app with codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=4, confirmed the generated Release app, DMG app, ZIP app, and installed /Applications/Parrot.app all report designated => identifier "com.example.parrot", confirmed the DMG app has Sealed Resources version 2 and CodeResources, confirmed spctl still rejects only because there is no Developer ID signature, and unzipped the ZIP artifact to verify the packaged app signature. Real System Settings inspection on 2026-06-23 confirmed two Parrot.app Screen Recording entries from old cdhash identities, matching the reported restart-still-unauthorized symptom.

features.description: RC implementation starts from an explicit release lane decision and records the resulting signing, notarization, bundle identifier, asset, and verification posture.
acceptance: Confirm whether the RC lane is unsigned public RC or public signed release.
Record bundle identifier, signing state, notarization state, release channel copy, target asset type, and required verification commands.
Record unsigned-lane downstream copy requirements for README, About, Release Notes, and update copy without marking those later gates complete.
Keep public signed release inputs out of scope until the user changes the lane.
Do not change the bundle identifier, signing team, deployment target, or app version as part of the lane record.
features.notes: User confirmed the RC lane in chat on 2026-07-04 as unsigned public RC (`还是使用未签名发布`). Recorded release posture: keep PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER=com.example.parrot, DEVELOPMENT_TEAM empty, no Developer ID signing, no notarization/stapling, unsigned/ad-hoc local signing only, and publish clearly as an unsigned RC with Gatekeeper limitations. Target assets remain .dmg, .zip, SHA256SUMS.txt, and GitHub-style RELEASE_NOTES.md from Scripts/package-release.sh --allow-untagged or the formal release path described in Docs/release-process.md. Required verification remains ./init.sh, Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh, Scripts/package-release.sh --allow-untagged, shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS.txt in the generated Dist directory, and codesign verification for generated app bundles. Public signed release inputs are out of scope unless the user changes the lane. Downstream unsigned copy requirements for README, About, Release Notes, update copy, and user-facing Gatekeeper guidance remain separate RC gates.

features.description: Settings validates OpenAI-compatible provider configuration before persisting non-secret settings or reporting a successful save/test.
acceptance: Save rejects a Base URL that cannot normalize to an HTTPS chat completions endpoint.
Save rejects an empty or whitespace-only model name.
Invalid Save attempts do not display `Settings saved` and do not persist invalid provider settings.
Test Connection uses the same validation before continuing and leaves the last valid provider settings intact when validation fails.
Legal root, /v1, and full /chat/completions HTTPS Base URLs still save successfully.
API keys remain Keychain-only; provider validation does not introduce secret persistence outside Keychain.
features.notes: ProviderSettingsStore.persistNonSecretSettings now validates currentSettings with ProviderEndpointNormalizer.chatCompletionsURL(from:) and a non-empty trimmed model name before encoding settings to UserDefaults. Save Settings and Test Connection already share persistNonSecretSettings, so non-HTTPS or otherwise non-normalizable Base URLs and empty model names fail before invalid settings can overwrite the stored provider record. The missing-model user-facing message now covers both saving and testing. Scripts/provider-endpoint-timeout-e2e.swift now asserts non-HTTPS Save and empty-model Save show errors, never show `Settings saved`, and write no provider settings; valid root, /v1, and full /chat/completions HTTPS URLs still save; invalid Test Connection attempts leave the previous valid settings intact. Verified with Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh on 2026-07-04; the only warnings were existing kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail and CGWindowListCreateImage deprecations. No Keychain storage code or API key persistence path was changed.

features.description: Translation failure states show a structured, safe error and one contextual recovery action in the affected translation window.
acceptance: Quick Text provider errors display a structured title, explanation, recovery suggestion, and contextual CTA.
Screenshot Translation provider errors use the same structured error presentation instead of only a raw status string.
Missing API Key and Keychain re-entry failures route to `Open Setup`.
Invalid Base URL, missing model, authentication failure, generic provider failure, and unsupported provider response route to `Open Model Settings`.
Network failures and request timeouts keep users in the translation window with `Retry`.
Error UI continues to sanitize provider messages and does not leak API keys, Bearer tokens, or token-like text.
CTA actions open existing local Settings/Setup windows or retry the current request; they do not record source text, provider responses, screenshots, or API keys.
features.notes: Read DESIGN.md for this UI-facing gate and kept the implementation native and low-noise: the existing ParrotStatusBanner now supports one compact bordered action button, without adding menu items or decorative surfaces. UserFacingErrorPresentation now carries a UserFacingErrorRecoveryAction: missing API key and Keychain re-entry route to Open Setup; invalid Base URL, missing model, authentication failure, generic provider failure, and unexpected provider responses route to Open Model Settings; network failures and timeouts route to Retry. QuickTextTranslationView and ScreenshotSelectionResultView both render provider failures as title/message/recovery banners with the mapped CTA. AppDelegate now passes existing Setup and Model Settings routes into both windows. ScreenshotTranslationComparisonStore now keeps structured errorPresentation state like Quick Text. The Quick Text large-text confirmation status now renders as a warning instead of a success banner. Scripts/provider-endpoint-timeout-e2e.swift now asserts the CTA mapping and verifies authentication error text redaction. Verified with Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh and ./init.sh on 2026-07-04; warnings were the known kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail, CGWindowListCreateImage, and Xcode/CoreSimulator noise. No Keychain storage, history persistence, screenshot persistence, provider request payload, menu structure, bundle identifier, signing, or release version behavior changed.

features.description: Settings exposes About, privacy, diagnostics, release notes, and feedback information without adding low-frequency items to the menu bar.
acceptance: Settings includes an About section reachable from the Settings sidebar.
About shows app version, build number, bundle identifier, macOS requirement, and unsigned RC release channel.

About includes privacy copy aligned with current implementation: API Key in Keychain, local OCR, only recognized or typed text sent to the configured provider, and text-only history that can be disabled or cleared.
About provides Open Release Notes, Copy Diagnostics Summary, and Send Feedback actions.
Diagnostics summary includes only version, build, bundle id, macOS version, provider preset id, release channel, permission status, and feature flags.

Diagnostics summary excludes API keys, Bearer tokens, token-like text, provider endpoint hosts, model names, source text, provider responses, history content, screenshots, window titles, and source app names.
No About, Release Notes, or Feedback item is added to the menu-bar menu.
features.notes: Read DESIGN.md and the RC PRD About/Privacy/Feedback requirements before editing. ProviderSettingsView now adds Settings > About after Privacy, with restrained native SwiftUI layout, semantic colors, existing Parrot panels/banners, no new menu items, and no new custom color/font/radius/motion decisions. About displays the running bundle version, build, bundle identifier, macOS 14.0+ requirement, and `Unsigned RC` release channel. It includes an unsigned Gatekeeper warning, privacy summary for Keychain-only API keys, local OCR, recognized/typed text-only provider requests, and local text-only history. Open Release Notes opens https://github.com/luzhengli/parrot/releases; Send Feedback opens https://github.com/luzhengli/parrot/issues/new; Copy Diagnostics Summary copies the allowlisted ParrotDiagnosticsSummary. ParrotDiagnosticsSummary only emits version, build, bundle id, macOS version, provider preset id, release channel, screen recording permission status, and static feature flags. Scripts/provider-endpoint-timeout-e2e.swift now verifies diagnostics include required allowlisted fields and exclude endpoint host, model name, API-key-like text, Bearer token text, source text, provider responses, history content, and screenshots. Verified with Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh and ./init.sh on 2026-07-04; known warnings remain kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail, CGWindowListCreateImage, and Xcode/CoreSimulator noise. No Keychain behavior, history persistence, screenshot persistence, provider payloads, menu structure, bundle identifier, signing team, deployment target, or version settings changed.

features.notes: README.md was replaced with current user-facing RC documentation: unsigned release candidate status, features, requirements, default shortcuts (Cmd+Shift+T, Cmd+Shift+2, Cmd+Option+,), install and Gatekeeper steps, first-run provider setup, provider endpoint rules, Keychain-only API key storage, local OCR, recognized/typed text-only provider requests, local text-only history, diagnostics exclusions, FAQ for Gatekeeper, Screen Recording, duplicate permission entries, Keychain re-entry, data location, uninstall, known limitations, developer verification, and Release configuration source. The old early-scaffold/core-under-development copy and hard-coded README version 0.1.0 were removed; README now says Release builds read version/build from Config/Release.xcconfig and records the current Release config as 0.1.4 build 6. features.description: Settings Setup acts as a lightweight versioned onboarding guide for first install, invalid provider setup, shortcuts, first translation, and optional screenshot OCR permission.
acceptance: Fresh install or current schema not-started state presents Settings > Setup on launch when provider configuration is otherwise valid.
Invalid provider configuration always presents Setup/Onboarding on launch, even when onboarding was skipped or completed for the current schema.
Onboarding includes steps for welcome/privacy, provider configuration, connection test, shortcut discovery, first Quick Text translation, and optional Screenshot OCR permission.
Users can skip onboarding for the current schema, mark it complete, reset it, and reopen it from Settings > Setup.
Skipped or completed onboarding for the current schema does not auto-reopen while provider configuration remains valid; older schema state reopens.

Onboarding state persists locally as status/schema metadata only and does not store API keys, provider endpoints, model names, user translation text, provider responses, screenshots, or diagnostics content.
The menu-bar menu remains short and does not add a persistent Setup or Onboarding item.
features.notes: Read DESIGN.md and the RC PRD onboarding requirements before editing. ProviderSettings.swift now defines ParrotOnboardingState with current schema version, notStarted/skipped/completed status, local UserDefaults persistence, invalid-provider override, same-schema skip/complete suppression, schema-change reopening, and reset support. AppDelegate launch gating now opens Settings > Setup when provider setup is invalid or onboarding should present, while the old persistent Setup Checklist menu item is removed to keep the menu short. Settings > Setup now starts with a native onboarding guide covering welcome/privacy, Model provider setup, Test Connection, Shortcuts, first Quick Text translation, and optional Screenshot OCR permission; actions use existing app entry points and local state. Users can Mark Complete when provider setup is ready, Skip for This Version, and Reset Guide. Scripts/provider-endpoint-timeout-e2e.swift now verifies fresh install, invalid provider override, skipped/current-schema suppression, completed/current-schema suppression, older-schema reopening, and reset behavior with isolated UserDefaults. Verified Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh and ./init.sh on 2026-07-04; also ran ./init.sh --run, confirmed the fixed Debug app process from .DerivedData, confirmed codesign designated requirement identifier com.example.parrot, and source-checked that the menu exposes Quick Text Translation, Screenshot Translation, Translation History, Pause Shortcuts, Settings, and Quit Parrot without a Setup Checklist item. features.description: Launch Hub provides a foreground startup entry after setup/onboarding are satisfied, with a persisted startup-display preference and Settings recovery path.
acceptance: Startup routing prioritizes invalid provider setup and onboarding before Launch Hub, so Setup/Onboarding and Launch Hub do not both open for the same launch decision.
When provider setup is valid, onboarding is skipped or completed for the current schema, and Show Launch Hub on Startup is enabled, startup routes to Launch Hub.
Users can disable Launch Hub startup from Launch Hub with Don't Show on Startup.
Settings includes a Launch section with Show Launch Hub on Startup and Open Launch Hub controls so users can restore the startup entry.
Launch Hub provides entry buttons for Quick Text, Screenshot OCR, History, Settings, Onboarding Guide, and About/Feedback, and displays the current core shortcuts.
Launch Hub routes update and feedback discovery to Settings > About without adding a menu-bar update item or implying automatic installation.
Launch Hub does not store translation text, screenshots, history content, API keys, provider endpoints, model names, window titles, menu-bar layout, notch state, screen size, or diagnostics content.
The menu-bar menu remains short and does not add a persistent Launch Hub item.
Dock icon activation-policy switching remains deferred to the later Dock icon gate.
features.notes: Read DESIGN.md and the RC PRD Launch Hub/startup-state requirements before editing. Added ParrotLaunchHubPreferences with default-on Show Launch Hub on Startup persistence and ParrotStartupPresentation to centralize startup priority: invalid provider or onboarding setup returns Setup first; otherwise enabled launch preference returns Launch Hub; disabled preference returns none. AppDelegate now calls this startup router after the status item and shortcuts are ready, presents Launch Hub with accessory activation, makeKey/orderFront, and orderFrontRegardless on startup, and keeps existing Setup/Settings/Quick Text/Screenshot/History routes. LaunchHubView is a compact native SwiftUI window with Quick Text, Screenshot OCR, History, Settings, Onboarding Guide, and Updates & Feedback actions plus current shortcut display and Don't Show on Startup; Updates & Feedback opens Settings > About, where the real update check lives. ProviderSettingsView now includes Settings > Launch with Show Launch Hub on Startup, Open Launch Hub, Reset Startup Display, and entry-point shortcut rows. The hub does not add a menu-bar update item, and it does not implement Dock icon activation-policy switching, which remains a separate later gate. Scripts/provider-endpoint-timeout-e2e.swift now verifies default launch preference, invalid-provider override, fresh valid-provider onboarding priority, skipped/completed current-schema Launch Hub routing, disabled startup quiet routing, re-enabled routing, and older-schema Setup priority with isolated UserDefaults. Verified Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh and ./init.sh on 2026-07-04; escalated ./init.sh --run launched the fixed Debug app; pgrep confirmed the running process is .DerivedData/Build/Products/Debug/Parrot.app/Contents/MacOS/Parrot; codesign -dr confirmed identifier com.example.parrot. WindowServer returned count=0 for all window queries and osascript Accessibility was blocked in this automation profile, so direct window-title observation was unavailable; this is recorded as a GUI-smoke limitation rather than a Launch Hub-specific failure. No API Key storage, provider payloads, screenshot persistence, history persistence, menu item list, bundle identifier, signing team, deployment target, or release version changed.

features.description: Screenshot Translation opens immediately after selection with a local OCR loading state, then updates to OCR success/failure and starts the existing translation flow only while the window is still active.
acceptance: After screenshot selection, AppDelegate presents Screenshot Translation immediately with Recognizing text locally status instead of blocking on Vision OCR.
Vision OCR runs off the main startup path and completion updates the Original pane only when the view task has not been cancelled.
OCR success loads the recognized text into the editable Original pane and automatically enters the existing translation chain.
OCR failure or no text keeps a readable state with New Screenshot available and no translation request.
Closing the window or starting a new screenshot prevents late OCR completion or translation callbacks from updating the closed window or writing successful history.
The loading, success, failure, and empty states preserve the privacy boundary: screenshots are processed locally, not uploaded, not saved to history, and screenshot geometry is not persisted.
Existing OCR source editing, provider prompt, language/style/prompt/glossary, request lifecycle, and text-only history tests remain passing.
features.notes: Read DESIGN.md and the RC PRD async OCR loading requirements before editing. ScreenshotPipelineStatus now has recognizing/success/failure phases and a Recognizing text locally status with local/no-upload privacy copy. AppDelegate no longer calls Vision OCR before creating the result window; it presents ScreenshotSelectionResultView immediately with .recognizing and passes an async OCR closure. ScreenshotOCRPipeline.receive is async, prepares the CGImage, runs VNRecognizeTextRequest from a user-initiated detached task, and updates its in-memory lastRecognizedText only when the parent task was not cancelled. ScreenshotSelectionResultView now owns OCR startup through its SwiftUI task, renders ProgressView/ParrotStatusPill loading, updates status only if the task is still active, loads successful OCR text into the editable source store, and then calls translateIfNeeded. Closing or New Screenshot cancels the view task and existing translation store, so late OCR/translation does not update the closed window or write success history. OCR failure/no-text still shows the existing New Screenshot recovery path. Scripts/ocr-source-text-editing-e2e.swift now asserts the recognizing phase is not treated as success and preserves the no-upload privacy message; Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh passed, covering OCR source editing, request lifecycle, provider endpoint/timeout, language/style/prompt/glossary, long text, keychain, and floating-window regressions. ./init.sh passed; escalated ./init.sh --run launched the fixed Debug app; pgrep confirmed the running process is .DerivedData/Build/Products/Debug/Parrot.app/Contents/MacOS/Parrot; codesign -dr confirmed identifier com.example.parrot. Real screenshot GUI selection could not be observed in this automation profile because global event/window introspection remains limited and WindowServer returned count=0 for all window queries; this is recorded as a GUI-smoke limitation. No API Key storage, provider payload content, screenshot upload, screenshot image persistence, history schema, menu item list, bundle identifier, signing team, deployment target, or release version changed.

features.description: Settings > About provides a manual GitHub Releases update check with up-to-date, update-available, and unable-to-check states for the unsigned RC lane.
acceptance: Users can trigger Check for Updates from Settings > About.
Launch Hub routes update discovery to Settings > About without adding a menu-bar update item.
The checker uses the running bundle version/build for comparison and does not hard-code the current app version.
Update feed parsing supports GitHub Releases latest response fields for version tag, release date, notes URL, download asset, prerelease flag, bounded summary, and checksum asset detection.
Current version equal to or higher than latest reports Up to Date and does not prompt downgrade.
Latest version higher than current reports Update Available with version, date, release summary, Open Release Notes, Download Update, and Copy Version Info.
Network, HTTP, invalid feed, or version parsing failures report Unable to Check without affecting Quick Text, Screenshot OCR, History, Settings, or Provider configuration.
Download Update opens the browser only; no automatic install, background update, Sparkle flow, or silent replacement is implied.
Unsigned RC copy states manual download and warns when checksum/signature metadata is not present in the feed.
The update check does not send API keys, provider endpoint/model settings, translation text, history content, screenshots, or diagnostics summary.
features.notes: Read DESIGN.md and the RC PRD Check for Updates requirements before editing. Added ParrotUpdateChecker, ParrotSemanticVersion, ParrotReleaseInfo, and ParrotUpdateCheckStatus in ProviderSettings.swift. The checker uses https://api.github.com/repos/luzhengli/parrot/releases/latest, sets a Parrot User-Agent with the current runtime version/channel, parses tag_name/html_url/published_at/body/prerelease/assets, chooses DMG or ZIP assets for manual download, detects checksum/SHA256 assets when present, bounds the release summary, and compares SemVer against CFBundleShortVersionString plus CFBundleVersion passed from the About view. Settings > About now has an Updates panel with idle/checking/up-to-date/update-available/unable-to-check states, a manual Check button, Open Release Notes, Download Update, and Copy Version Info. Launch Hub's release shortcut now says Updates & Feedback and routes to Settings > About; the menu bar still has no update item. The UI explicitly keeps unsigned RC updates manual and does not imply automatic install or verified replacement when checksum/signature metadata is absent. Scripts/provider-endpoint-timeout-e2e.swift now verifies version parsing/comparison, bounded summary, prerelease preservation, DMG asset selection, checksum asset detection, fake successful update check, local-newer up-to-date behavior, and invalid-feed Unable to Check without network. Verified Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh and ./init.sh on 2026-07-04; escalated ./init.sh --run launched the fixed Debug app; pgrep confirmed the running process is .DerivedData/Build/Products/Debug/Parrot.app/Contents/MacOS/Parrot; codesign -dr confirmed identifier com.example.parrot. Real About window interaction and live GitHub network check were not performed in this automation profile; parser and state behavior are covered with injected fake responses. No API Key storage, provider payloads, screenshot persistence, history persistence, menu item list, bundle identifier, signing team, deployment target, or release version changed.

The confirmation explains that only local text records are deleted.
The confirmation states that API keys, provider settings, screenshots, and app preferences are not deleted.
Cancel preserves existing history records.
Confirm clears the local text history records and shows a short completion status.
Disabling history still preserves existing records until the user explicitly clears them.

The change does not alter history schema, provider settings, Keychain behavior, screenshot persistence, app preferences, menu structure, bundle identifier, signing, deployment target, or release version.

features.description: Core Parrot surfaces provide independent page-level Always on Top controls backed by per-surface preferences and NSWindow.Level.
acceptance: Quick Text has a visible Always on Top pin button before the History button.
Screenshot Translation and screenshot-capture error surfaces have a visible Always on Top pin button, with the Screenshot Translation button before the History button.
History has its own Always on Top pin button.
Settings has its own Always on Top pin button, and the About/Update section uses an independent About preference when active.
Each surface persists to its own local preference key: quickText.alwaysOnTop, screenshotTranslation.alwaysOnTop, history.alwaysOnTop, settings.alwaysOnTop, and about.alwaysOnTop.
Toggling one surface does not change other surfaces' persisted state.
Saved pin state restores by setting only the current NSWindow.Level to floating or normal; it does not change app activation policy, global shortcuts, menu structure, or other windows.
The screenshot selection overlay keeps its temporary screen-saver-level behavior and does not gain a persistent pin preference.
Pin controls provide state feedback through icon tint/background plus tooltip and accessibility label/value.
The change does not persist screenshots, screenshot geometry, window titles, source app names, API keys, provider settings, translation text, history content, or diagnostics content.

features.description: Quick Text copy explains multiline input and large-text confirmation in user-facing terms.

features.notes: Added a native AppKit full-screen overlay for region selection. Added Screen Recording preflight/request before snapshotting, because macOS can return a desktop-only image when permission is missing. A release-package regression showed CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess can present the macOS Screen Recording prompt while returning false for the current attempt; the app now suppresses the Parrot error window only for the first request-presented state, then shows Parrot's actionable permission guidance on a later attempt if Screen Recording is still not granted. Follow-up Debug workflow fix: ./init.sh now ad-hoc signs the built Debug app bundle with the same stable designated requirement as the unsigned release package, identifier "com.example.parrot", after signing Debug-only nested dylibs. This prevents ./init.sh --run builds from using per-build cdhash TCC identities that do not match /Applications/Parrot.app. Verified ./init.sh Debug build/signing, codesign --verify --deep --strict, codesign -dr - reporting identifier "com.example.parrot" for both the Debug app and installed /Applications/Parrot.app, source-linked screen-capture access gate E2E, and a real Debug app smoke where Cmd+Shift+2 opened the Screenshot Translation permission window only because this machine has not granted Screen Recording to Parrot. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: screenshot result, OCR failure, permission error, and selection overlay visual feedback were refined without changing local OCR, screenshot upload, or image persistence behavior. features.description: API keys are stored in macOS Keychain and never written to repository files or logs.
acceptance: Save API Key to Keychain.

features.notes: KeychainSecretStore is implemented and integrated with settings save, test, replace, and delete flows. API Keys are isolated by provider account suffix so DeepSeek, GLM, OpenAI, and Custom can use different secrets. The app stores only a non-secret provider setup record in UserDefaults, uses that record for Settings/onboarding/missing-key decisions, and never probes Keychain just to decide whether an API Key exists. Quick Text and Screenshot OCR translation now check the setup record before reading any API Key, and readAPIKey also checks the setup record before returning a process-cached secret. Successful saves refresh an in-memory process cache. Translation reads otherwise combine LAContext.interactionNotAllowed=true with kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail so macOS Keychain password prompts are disabled during translation. If a previous debug-build Keychain item still requires system UI, Parrot clears that provider setup record and cached secret, then shows an in-app re-enter-key/missing-key message, so retries do not keep touching Keychain. Repository scan found no token-like API secret values in tracked source. Verified ./init.sh Debug build and Scripts/keychain-cache-e2e.swift covering no-setup no-read, Settings no-read, save/cache/delete, setup-record-gates-cache, authentication-UI-fail enforcement, unsafe-record cleanup, and retry-after-cleanup no-read. 2026-07-04 adversarial verification: keychain-cache-e2e passed after updating the fake Keychain assertion for the current SDK, where LAContext.interactionNotAllowed does not read back as true even after assignment. Product code still stores API Keys only in Keychain and keeps secrets out of defaults/history/docs. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity UI pass reverified privacy boundary with keychain-cache-e2e after Settings visual changes. API Keys remain Keychain-only; Settings form refactor did not add secret persistence, logs, or defaults storage.

features.notes: Implemented QuickTextTranslationView and wired the existing Quick Text Translation menu/global shortcut entry to a real keyboard-first input window. The window auto-focuses a native NSTextView, Enter triggers translation through the configured OpenAI-compatible provider, Esc closes, Cmd+K clears, and Cmd+Enter copies the translated text and closes after a successful result. Follow-up fix changed translation from a one-shot /chat/completions request to OpenAI-compatible SSE streaming so Translation receives text deltas as they arrive; the ready message is only set after the stream finishes, and errors clear partial output and show the exception message. A repeated empty Translation-area report showed the first follow-up was incomplete: the result was still rendered by a dynamic SwiftUI ScrollView/Text inside a too-small fixed window, so the same StateObject data could appear only after a later window relayout. The result area now uses a fixed-height AppKit read-only NSTextView that is updated directly from translatedText, and the quick translation window height is increased to fit the result area. Verified ./init.sh Debug build and checked no other Swift caller still uses the old non-streaming translate path. User local E2E verification on 2026-06-22 confirmed Cmd+Shift+T opens the quick translation window, text input translates with streaming output in the Translation area, the ready message appears only after translation completes, and Cmd+Enter copies the translation and closes the window. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: Quick Text now has a clearer native header, compact language controls, distinct input/result panes, unified status banners, and a stable footer action bar; keyboard handlers and streaming translation path are unchanged. acceptance: Missing non-secret API Key setup record or invalid provider settings opens a Setup checklist on launch.
Checklist guides the user to API Key setup, connection test, shortcuts, and Screen Recording explanation.
Quick Text remains usable without Screen Recording permission.
Configured users are not interrupted every launch.
Checklist does not store API Keys in UserDefaults or request screenshot permission proactively.
features.notes: Added Settings > Setup with native checklist rows for API Key setup record, Provider endpoint/model validity, connection test, shortcuts, and Screen Recording. AppDelegate adds a Setup Checklist menu item and opens Setup on launch only when the selected provider lacks the non-secret API setup record or provider endpoint/model is invalid. Screen Recording status uses CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess only and the button opens System Settings; it does not force authorization. Verification: ./init.sh and ./init.sh --run passed, and feature_list JSON validation passed. Full GUI shortcut/menu smoke was not attempted beyond launch because this automation session has known CGEvent/AX delivery limitations from prior runs.

features.description: Long text is segmented sequentially with progress, cancellation, failure recovery, and history gating.
acceptance: Text under the single-request threshold keeps the current single streaming request path.
Text from 2000-8000 characters uses paragraph-first sequential segmented translation.
Text above 8000 characters requires explicit confirmation before any provider request is sent.
Progress shows the current segment count, for example Translating 2/6.
Cancel stops remaining segments and does not write success history.
A failed segment preserves completed UI text and Retry continues from the failed segment.
Markdown, code blocks, URLs, variables, and paragraph structure are preserved as far as the deterministic segmenter allows.
features.notes: Added LongTextTranslation.swift with 2500 character single-request threshold, 8000 character automatic segmented limit, 1800 character segment target, paragraph/code-fence-aware splitting, and explicit over-limit confirmation. Quick Text and Screenshot Translation use the planner; segmented mode runs sequentially and updates progress as Translating n/m. Verification: long-text-translation-e2e covers single path, 2000-8000 segmentation, code block and URL preservation, over-8000 confirmation, and confirmed large-text segmentation. translation-request-lifecycle-e2e covers segmented failure retry and no history write for partial/canceled results. Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh, ./init.sh, and ./init.sh --run passed.

features.description: V2.0 establishes first-stage engineering seams and a repeatable core E2E entry for pure logic and source-linked integration tests.
acceptance: Provider endpoint/timeout/error handling, request lifecycle, and long-text splitting are separated into focused source files.
Translation stores accept fake streaming providers and history recorders for deterministic tests.
A single stable command runs core source-linked E2E tests.
Existing Keychain, prompt, glossary, OCR source editing, language, and style tests remain passing.
No Provider request, Keychain, OCR, history, window default, or shortcut behavior is intentionally changed by the seam extraction.
features.notes: Added ProviderEndpoint.swift, TranslationRequestLifecycle.swift, LongTextTranslation.swift, FloatingWindowPlacement.swift, and Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh. QuickTextTranslationStore and ScreenshotTranslationComparisonStore now accept TranslationClientFactory and historyRecorder injections so fake providers can verify lifecycle behavior without real network or real history writes. The first-stage split intentionally avoided a large same-turn move of all Keychain, Prompt, Glossary, and Provider client code out of ProviderSettings.swift; those deeper mechanical moves remain a lower-risk follow-up now that seams and tests exist. Verification: Scripts/run-core-e2e.sh passed and includes provider-endpoint-timeout, long-text, translation-request-lifecycle, keychain-cache, translation-language-controls, translation-style, custom-translation-prompt, terminology-glossary, floating-window-position-preferences, and ocr-source-text-editing E2Es. ./init.sh and ./init.sh --run passed.
